so for GrowthX, generally, we're a growth agency for our clients.

it looks anything from just typical SEO blog content, like a listicle or something like that, to things that are much more specialized, like for Webflow, for example, which is one of my clients, we do like these like pretty deep dive integration docs for them.

have a community called AI-led growth where the whole goal is like, you know, it's probably like one of the biggest changes in how people do work.

mean, I guess, like, just in general, kind of your expertise, what you've been working on, maybe a little bit about, like, what you're interested in as well.

I started as a senior content strategist.

And just learning by doing, um, figuring out how we can go from.

We go through interviews and or, you know, now we're working to incorporate more stuff like podcasts and webinars and blog posts and white papers.

Anything that's not, you know, out there on the Internet already.

then I'm building the, like, he does the more technical side of things to be transparent.

was a lot of like writing post manually to help teach it and give it an idea of what we're looking for in terms of voice and tone and structure and all sorts of stuff like that.

So it's been interesting to kind of work with it and figure.

was talking to Natalie a little bit about that.

then two, can we teach other people and bring them along this journey of, hey, we're figuring this out.

I wasn't super confident in...
I in...

And I was a little worried, like, am I even going to be able to find something?
Like, is it something that I want to do?
So it really made me question, like, what is the role of people in content marketing now that we can automate so much of it?
And so I found that very interesting now to like figure out what that is.
And it's clear that like AI is a huge part of content marketing.

I always tell people that I speak to about this, like I started off doing freelance writing and the stuff I was doing was like five reasons you should buy bulk salt or like, you know, like the best ways to clear your driveway in the winter.
One of them is bulk, like just like the super, you know, keyword focused garbage content, to be honest.
But now, like, I'm, you know, I'm fortunate that I had that kind of stepping stone in my in my career.
That doesn't exist anymore.
Like, I think that AI.
AI is so much better than so many freelancers out there, even really good freelancers sometimes.
The content that comes out of AI is better than that.
And so it's clear that the whole landscape is changing so much.
And it's interesting to figure out where it all fits in and how it works with human input or as you transition more to an automated approach.
Yeah, I mean, I think I've been trying to figure out what the content strategy almost for our community is.
And I feel like it's going to be quite broad, like everything from like at the top end, there's going to be like lots of high production, high investment sort of like courses and workshops, right?
Like we're, you know, probably very little AI is used.
I mean, like, I guess.
For us, it's like just supernaturally, use it constantly, but like not in terms of like writing end-to-end.
And then, yeah, there's going to be a ton of repurposing.
Like I guess the way we're generally working is like the video is kind of the core.
And then that becomes the thing that gets repurposed.

And then I guess some of the writing is like a derivative of it.
And then some of the writing is almost like seeded by the video.
And then you'd have to like go and do research and write more.

Yeah, that's how I'm thinking about it.
Like we definitely have a ton on the like high polish, high investment type content, but then we also want to experiment more in other areas.
It's almost like treat ourselves as a client, you know.
Yeah, exactly.
And like spin up the same workflows, the same kind of like structures to create content at scale and then kind of use ourselves as a guinea pig, you know.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.

Like whether it was like a client or again, like writing for yourself that like feels similar, like it becomes like teaching people about it.
Relatively new topic.

Yeah, I mean, what I'm doing now, find, like, building out the documentation and the use cases for the thing that we're building, it's not ready yet, but just in the process of kind of building out, like, how I use it, how it can be used, how to get it more, you know, how to make it useful for different team members who might have a different use case than I do, just so they can see the value in it and hopefully become more efficient as well.

Have you found, just like, even in your own learning or trying to teach people on your team that aren't, like, the most technical, like, on how to use AI, like, any, I guess, challenges or, like, learnings from that process?
Yeah, for sure.
I mean, people are not as, like, thorough as they need to be or, like, as explicit when they're prompting things.
You know, when we're speaking about just, like, working with Claude or something like that, I think I take a lot of, like, that knowledge for granted.
It's, like, you know, the questions that I ask it are things that I ask it to do.
The way that I phrase it is because I've learned by doing that, like, you know, asking me just like, hey, build me, take this and analyze it and build voice and tone guidelines.
Like, that's not enough.
It's going to spew out like a 12-page paper.
And it's just like, how do you, like, what's the nuance there to give it enough detail to, like, get it to where you want?
Because I feel like a lot of people will just be like, do this for me.
And then it puts it out and then they go, okay, great, sounds good.
But there's not really much of an analysis there.
They don't, I think there's, like, a lack of evaluation of what the outputs are.
And with that, they aren't diving deeper into, like, how should they get, how should they be more specific in the prompts that they put into it?
Do you guys try to teach them how to do that?
Yeah, that's been a learning in my, like, documentation as well.
Instead of saying, like, step one, do this, it's like, here's an example prompt with the level of detail that I use.
very Thank
To make sure that they're putting in something similar.
even if the use case is a bit different, making sure they're giving it enough context and enough detail in that input, as opposed to just blindly following that step.
Do you guys try to teach a little bit more on the internals of LMs, or just trying to go a bit deeper on the technology itself, or is it mostly just on how to apply it for your use case?

any questions that you...

So I guess our vision is the community is the marketing for what we do.
Yeah.

And if you kind of don't learn how to use AI.
Yeah.

And also as a company, you're probably not growing as fast.

Or, I mean, some of the, honestly, it'll just be like SEO topics.
Like, do you come up with a good content strategy?
You know, that's the best way to do that if you want to leverage like AI, you know, like don't just do a keyword gap analysis using some SEO tools.
Like think about your customers and the use cases and like break things down, you know, first principles, like kind of like evangelizing the right way to kind of do growth, essentially.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And some of will look like writing, some of it will look like, you know, facilitating the right people from our team or even externally to create that content, like a video.
So I'm really personally inspired by the deep learning.ai.
don't know if you've ever been on that website, but it's the founder of Coursera, like Andrew Ng.

He's like very deep into AI and he like, I mean, honestly, it kind of is starting to look like Coursera again, but like very specialized to AI and like some of the things he did really well early on was like, Hey, I'm going to do a course on, you know, like agentic workflows and I'm going to bring the founder of, you know, like the agentic workflow company.
To teach this course, you know, like I'm going to teach you on coding agents and I'm going to bring the VP of product from Anthropic to do that.
I think like I would love to do more stuff like that.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss the Managing Editor role for GrowthX's AI-led growth community and publication.](https://fathom.video/share/jby4LZPCNajFBjL95rfvtiVdLuGD-hVC?tab=summary&timestamp=996.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX is building an educational community and content platform focused on AI adoption for non-technical roles in marketing, sales, and operations](https://fathom.video/share/jby4LZPCNajFBjL95rfvtiVdLuGD-hVC?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
